evals.name: JSON Manifest dispatch pipeline
evals.prompt: User issues verified command: 'EXECUTE_PLAN_V1: Formation(WY_LLC) -> Banking(Mercury) -> Wallet(Safe_Mainnet) -> Funding(Stripe_Connect)'. Trace the full manifest dispatch pipeline: how the command is cryptographically verified, how the Orchestrator parses the manifest, how sub-agents receive tasks, and what happens at each step.
evals.expected_output: Response must describe: cryptographic session verification; manifest parsing into sub-tasks; sub-agents receive tasks via secure channel; Legal Swarm forms WY LLC (parallel: KYC upload); Mobility Swarm applies to Mercury; Web3 Swarm deploys Safe{Mainnet} multi-sig; Capital Swarm connects Stripe Connect; each step has HITL checkpoint; Telegram confirmation required after each CRIMSON action.
assertions: Parsers manifest into sub-tasks per swarm
Legal Swarm forms WY LLC in parallel with KYC upload
Web3 Swarm deploys Safe{Mainnet} multi-sig wallet
Telegram confirmation required at CRIMSON steps

evals.name: CRIMSON multi-sig approval flow
evals.prompt: A $15,000 wire transfer is triggered from the Operating Fund to a newly formed UAE entity. Walk through the CRIMSON approval flow: how Watchdog detects it, what the multi-sig requirement is, how the 48-hour cooling-off period works, and what the user must do to authorize or abort.
evals.expected_output: Response must: detect CRIMSON (>$10k wire); require 2-of-3 multi-sig (user + Watchdog + Orchestrator); start 48hr cooling-off timer; send Telegram alert to user with transfer details; user must explicitly approve via Telegram green button; Wire transfer executes only after approval + cooling-off elapsed; abort available during cooling-off.
assertions: Sends Telegram alert with transfer details
User must explicitly approve via Telegram

evals.name: Idempotent execution on retry
evals.prompt: The Green Button command times out during entity formation (the Wyoming Secretary of State API is slow). The user retries. Explain how the Orchestrator ensures idempotency — that the LLC is not double-formed, that the KYC data is not re-submitted, and how the partial state is recovered.
evals.expected_output: Response must use idempotency key (entity_formation_${userId}_${timestamp}); check formation status before re-submitting; if LLC already exists (based on name+date), skip formation and verify existing documents; KYC data cached with 24hr TTL; partial state recovered from AgentDecisionLog; no duplicate charges or submissions.
assertions: Uses idempotency key for formation request
